MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Jul-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jamon L. Bollock, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP, Tel. 415.659.5623, for defendant and moving party;
Michael J. Van Zandt, Esq. of Hanson Bridgett LLP, Tel. 415.995.5001, with Kathryn Devincenzi, Esq., Tel. 415.221.4700, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT USA WASTE OF CALIFORNIA, INC.'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES is denied without prejudice per CC 1717, and Plaintiff's motion to tax is granted in its entirety. It is premature to determine the prevailing party because this case is still pending. Defendant's cases are distinguishable because they involve petitions to compel arbitration. Plaintiff's motion to enforce settlement and motion for reconsideration are not analogous to those cases. Under the instant circumstances, the motion to enforce settlement is not the type of discreet legal proceeding where fees should be awarded. The case is still open and generally there is only one prevailing party in an action. In Frog Creek Partners, LLC v. Vance Brown, Inc. (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 515, 543, the court notes that cases outside the arbitration context support the conclusion that there can only be one prevailing party. The language of section 12 of the parties' Settlement Agreement does not supersede CC 1717. See Presley of So. Cal. v. Whelan (1983) 146 Cal.App.3d 959 (no interim award of fees allowed even though the contract stated that the prevailing party in any proceeding relating to the contract shall be entitled to recover attorneys fees). One ORDER SIGNED for the 2 motions. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
